fields.name: Dhaka
fields.description: Capital and largest city of Bangladesh; political and economic hub with extremely high density.

fields.name: Chattogram
fields.description: Major port city and industrial centre; second-largest urban area in Bangladesh.

fields.name: Sylhet
fields.description: Northeast regional city; economic hub with tea agriculture and cultural heritage significance.

fields.name: Rajshahi
fields.description: Northern city known for education and silk production; regional commerce centre.

fields.name: Bogura
fields.description: Growing urban centre in the north; commercial and transit node.

fields.name: Khulna
fields.description: Industrial and port-adjacent city in the southwest; major jute processing and manufacturing industries.

fields.name: Comilla
fields.description: Historical and commercial city near the Indian border; transport hub.

fields.name: Barisal
fields.description: Riverine city in the south; administrative and inland water commerce centre.

fields.name: Mymensingh
fields.description: Administrative and educational centre; strong service and agriculture economies.

fields.name: Rangpur
fields.description: Northern regional capital; agrarian and trade hub with growing industry.

fields.name: Gazipur
fields.description: Rapidly expanding industrial suburb of Dhaka; significant manufacturing base.

fields.name: Narayanganj
fields.description: Part of Greater Dhaka; commercial port and textile centre.

fields.name: Tangail
fields.description: Large city; textiles and trade; commuter link to Dhaka.

fields.name: Sirajganj
fields.description: Riverine trade city on the Jamuna; commerce and grain markets.

fields.name: Pabna
fields.description: Historic market city with agricultural and light industrial activity.

fields.name: Chandpur
fields.description: City at the confluence of Padma and Meghna rivers; fishery and shipbuilding traditions.

fields.name: Feni
fields.description: Transit junction city near Chattogram; road and rail links to eastern Bangladesh.

fields.name: Dinajpur
fields.description: Northern city with grain markets and logistics; satellite industry.

fields.name: Jamalpur
fields.description: Trade and administrative node; agricultural service town.

fields.name: Satkhira
fields.description: Border city near Sundarbans; cross-border trade and fisheries.

fields.name: Kushtia
fields.description: Western city known for cultural heritage, riverside geography, and growing textile industry.

fields.name: Cox's Bazar
fields.description: Home to the world's longest natural sea beach, Cox's Bazar is the tourism capital of Bangladesh.
